title: Define the requirement - Standards and quality assurance | CanadaBuys

breadcrumbs.title: Importance of a clear procurement requirement
Completing the Security Requirements Check List
Security in standing offers and supply arrangements
Compliance with the Canadian electrical code

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: Standards and quality assurance

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: For the procurement of goods and services, recognized Canadian standards or specifications must be used. Unless the volume or specific nature of the purchase justifies an exception. If no Canadian standards are available, you must use wherever possible, Canadian specifications from a recognized standards-writing organization. In cases where neither is available, suitable US or international standards or specifications may be used.

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: When determining the suitability of US or international standards or specifications you should consult with the client, and may call on the assistance of the Canadian General Standards Board (CGSB). Your determination to use such standards should also reflect the extent to which:

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: Canadian views have been reflected in the standard or specification

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: products available in Canada are likely to meet the standard or specification

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the standard or specification is likely to discriminate against products

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: You must assess the adequacy and applicability of any standards, specifications, including client-developed specifications, or purchase descriptions included by a client in the requisition. When a requisition does not include an existing standard, specification or purchase description, that you consider appropriate, recommend that the requisition be amended for its inclusion.

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: Note : You must also identify the need for a new standard, specification or purchase description when a suitable one does not exist for a specific product or service.

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: Clients are responsible for clearly stating their Government Quality Assurance (GQA) requirements, including quality assurance and quality control on their requisition or attached documentation. The client statement should clearly and completely describe the technical requirements and the requirement for GQA , and must designate the inspection authority and the point of inspection.

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: The level of GQA needed will depend on the technical requirements of the contract and the performance history of the offeror. The GQA requirement may be specified in terms of:

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the quality standard against which verification will be conducted

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the requirement for the contractor to establish and maintain a quality assurance system

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the requirement for the offeror or contractor to demonstrate conformance

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: what quality verification activity will be done by the government

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: consignee inspection

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the requirement for the offeror or contractor to provide proof of compliance in accordance with an acceptable quality assurance standard or specification

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the requirement for the offeror or contractor to submit samples for approval, such as pre-award samples, first-off units, pre-production, qualification or sealed samples

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the requirement for the offeror or contractor to submit an inspection plan

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the requirement for the inspection authority to verify that the product supplied:

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: is equal in all respects to the product qualified during the Quality Products List or Qualification Program List (QPL) or Certification Program List (CPL) process

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: is manufactured under the same conditions as the product qualified during the QPL or CPL process

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: has adequate details of acceptance inspection, tests and trials

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: As an alternative, the offeror or contractor may be required to be listed in an acceptable, recognized qualifying program, such as International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 9001-2015, ISO 9001-2015 plus AQAP-2110, or equivalent, that provides for adequate audit and controls. When using this option it is essential to conduct offeror or contractor surveillance to ensure compliance with the specified standards. You must inform clients of the availability of CGSB or other listing programs that, if used for procurement, would reduce the need for GQA . For example, CGSB’s Certification and Qualification Programs and the Qualified Products Lists are operated on a cost recovery basis with no direct expense or use of the resources to the client.

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: If a requisition does not include a GQA requirement or the level of GQA is deemed insufficient, you must work with the client to develop an appropriate GQA framework. This includes advising clients on the financial and operational implications of appropriate GQA , for the client, the offeror or contractor and PSPC .

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: If a requisition does not specify an offeror or contractor quality system, it may be necessary for you to recommend that the client includes requirements and non-conformance could have significant implications for product safety, reliability, or operational consequence.

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: This is particularly important when the requirement involves:

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: newly designed products being produced to government-generated specifications

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: a product or service where current offerors have a history of not-conforming to specifications or previous similar requirements have resulted in chronic client complaints

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: a product of high technical complexity; a product has a stringent interchangeability requirement; or a critical product where non-conformance could result in system failure

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: the product or service being purchased for the first time and no history of performance is available

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: at least one potential offeror or contractor has a weak quality system

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: This is required to provide adequate protection for both the client and PSPC .

structure_blocks.heading: Standards and quality assurance
structure_blocks.text: You may also consider an offeror or contractor quality system if a requirement has a significant dollar value. However, issues relating to the nature of the requirement are usually more important than the dollar value.

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: Government Quality Assurance at source

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: You should use GQA at source when any of the following conditions apply:

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: the inspection authority is different from the consignee

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: the benefits justify the costs of conducting inspections at the source

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: the offeror or contractor has a history of subpar performance or quality and conditions preclude procurement from other sources

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: assessing conformance upon receipt isn't feasible due to:

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: the product containing critical characteristics not visible in the end item

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: special safety or security characteristics

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: risk of damage to special packing and packaging

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: delivery to multiple locations

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: inadequate conditions or capabilities at the destination

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: The GQA at source may be performed by an inspection authority specified by the client or by an inspection authority commissioned by PSPC on the client's behalf. As part of the inspection, contractor performance data respecting quality must be documented and copies of all inspection reports provided to the sector or region.

structure_blocks.heading: Government Quality Assurance at source
structure_blocks.text: Additionally, PSPC holds the authority to provide additional quality tasking for civilian marine services, such as conducting inspections and arranging for technical support.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Listing programs

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: You can expedite procurement by using listing programs. This establishes a list of pre-identified products or services that comply with recognized performance standards or specifications. These lists are developed and maintained by the CGSB and the Department of National Defence (DND).

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Listing or qualification programs are normally established in situations where:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: testing requirements could delay delivery

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: the costs of acceptance inspection are prohibitively high

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: assurance of product conformance or offeror capability is needed beforehand

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: complex testing equipment and procedures are required

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: there are regular purchases of products in large quantities

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Before contracting, you should verify which product or service offered has been approved with the standards (listing) organization. This signifies compliance with recognized standards or specifications, however, it does not relieve the contractor from meeting all specified contract requirements or guarantee acceptance under a contract.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Reasons that the responsible qualifying authority may discontinue the qualification and remove a product or service from an existing list include:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: changes in product formulation that impair product quality

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: production process adjustments that impair product quality

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: verified, authenticated field failures, which is attributable to non-conformance of the product to the relevant standard or specification (in contrast with perceived field failures which should be reported by users to the qualifying authority)

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: failure to meet verification test requirements

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: failure to submit samples for testing, where requested

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: failure to submit data for qualification maintenance, when requested

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: withdrawal for cause, such as the contractor has ceased operation, changed location or has consistently failed to respond to requests for quotation

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: a cancelled listing resulting from a cancelled governing standard, or a cancelled or superseded specification

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: When there are indications of non-conformance, and if PSPC and the client determine that a qualified offeror or contractor does not conform to the applicable standard, you must notify the qualifying authority. Offerors or contractors have the right to appeal against the removal as per the established appeal procedures.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: When utilizing a listing program for procurement, you must state in the notice of proposed procurement (NPP), solicitation of offers, and contract documents that the offeror or contractor and its product must be listed in the appropriate listing as detailed below.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: QPL includes:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: underlay

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: security guards

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: remanufactured toner cartridges

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: protective clothing

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: polyethylene vapor barrier

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: paints

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: office furniture

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: medical gloves

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Laboratory Acceptance Program

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: dockside Monitoring Company

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: carpets

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Canadian Non-Destructive Testing Personnel Certification Program

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Canadian Air Transport Security Authority screening contractors

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: breather type sheathing membrane

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: CPL includes:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: polyethylene vapor barrier

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: surgical and patient examination rubber gloves

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: breather type sheathing membrane

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: firefighter's protective clothing, protecting against heat and flame

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: fireline workwear for forest firefighters

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Laboratory Acceptance Program

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Registered Quality Systems List ISO 9001 (ISO 9000 Quality Management Systems):

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Lists of companies compliant with ISO 9001:2015 models for quality systems, marking the transition from ISO 9001:2008 to the current version.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Registered Environmental Management Systems List:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Lists of companies compliant with ISO 14001:2015 standard for environmental management systems.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Certified Occupational Health and Safety Standards (OHSAS) List:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Lists of companies compliant with OHSAS 18001:1999 specification for health and safety management systems.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Certified Food Safety Management Systems List: Alberta and Ontario Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) Advantage List (AHA!) Certification Program: the formalized process whereby a food safety management system is assessed against the AHA! standard of the Alberta Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Ontario HACCP Advantage Certification Program: the formalized process whereby a food safety management system is assessed against the Advantage HACCP standard of the Ontario Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs.

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: Certified Drinking Water Quality Systems (Ministry of Ontario) List:

structure_blocks.heading: Listing programs
structure_blocks.text: A registration program designed to recognize operating authorities managing municipal residential drinking water systems that meet the Ontario Drinking Water Quality Standard through accreditation by an independent third party.

raw_text:
Standards and quality assurance

For the procurement of goods and services, recognized Canadian standards or specifications must be used. Unless the volume or specific nature of the purchase justifies an exception. If no Canadian standards are available, you must use wherever possible, Canadian specifications from a recognized standards-writing organization. In cases where neither is available, suitable US or international standards or specifications may be used.

When determining the suitability of US or international standards or specifications you should consult with the client, and may call on the assistance of the Canadian General Standards Board (CGSB). Your determination to use such standards should also reflect the extent to which:

Canadian views have been reflected in the standard or specification

products available in Canada are likely to meet the standard or specification

the standard or specification is likely to discriminate against products

You must assess the adequacy and applicability of any standards, specifications, including client-developed specifications, or purchase descriptions included by a client in the requisition. When a requisition does not include an existing standard, specification or purchase description, that you consider appropriate, recommend that the requisition be amended for its inclusion.

Note : You must also identify the need for a new standard, specification or purchase description when a suitable one does not exist for a specific product or service.

Clients are responsible for clearly stating their Government Quality Assurance (GQA) requirements, including quality assurance and quality control on their requisition or attached documentation. The client statement should clearly and completely describe the technical requirements and the requirement for GQA , and must designate the inspection authority and the point of inspection.

The level of GQA needed will depend on the technical requirements of the contract and the performance history of the offeror. The GQA requirement may be specified in terms of:

the quality standard against which verification will be conducted

the requirement for the contractor to establish and maintain a quality assurance system

the requirement for the offeror or contractor to demonstrate conformance

what quality verification activity will be done by the government

consignee inspection

the requirement for the offeror or contractor to provide proof of compliance in accordance with an acceptable quality assurance standard or specification

the requirement for the offeror or contractor to submit samples for approval, such as pre-award samples, first-off units, pre-production, qualification or sealed samples

the requirement for the offeror or contractor to submit an inspection plan

the requirement for the inspection authority to verify that the product supplied:

is equal in all respects to the product qualified during the Quality Products List or Qualification Program List (QPL) or Certification Program List (CPL) process

is manufactured under the same conditions as the product qualified during the QPL or CPL process

has adequate details of acceptance inspection, tests and trials

As an alternative, the offeror or contractor may be required to be listed in an acceptable, recognized qualifying program, such as International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 9001-2015, ISO 9001-2015 plus AQAP-2110, or equivalent, that provides for adequate audit and controls. When using this option it is essential to conduct offeror or contractor surveillance to ensure compliance with the specified standards. You must inform clients of the availability of CGSB or other listing programs that, if used for procurement, would reduce the need for GQA . For example, CGSB’s Certification and Qualification Programs and the Qualified Products Lists are operated on a cost recovery basis with no direct expense or use of the resources to the client.

If a requisition does not include a GQA requirement or the level of GQA is deemed insufficient, you must work with the client to develop an appropriate GQA framework. This includes advising clients on the financial and operational implications of appropriate GQA , for the client, the offeror or contractor and PSPC .

If a requisition does not specify an offeror or contractor quality system, it may be necessary for you to recommend that the client includes requirements and non-conformance could have significant implications for product safety, reliability, or operational consequence.

This is particularly important when the requirement involves:

newly designed products being produced to government-generated specifications

a product or service where current offerors have a history of not-conforming to specifications or previous similar requirements have resulted in chronic client complaints

a product of high technical complexity; a product has a stringent interchangeability requirement; or a critical product where non-conformance could result in system failure

the product or service being purchased for the first time and no history of performance is available

at least one potential offeror or contractor has a weak quality system

This is required to provide adequate protection for both the client and PSPC .

You may also consider an offeror or contractor quality system if a requirement has a significant dollar value. However, issues relating to the nature of the requirement are usually more important than the dollar value.

Government Quality Assurance at source

You should use GQA at source when any of the following conditions apply:

the inspection authority is different from the consignee

the benefits justify the costs of conducting inspections at the source

the offeror or contractor has a history of subpar performance or quality and conditions preclude procurement from other sources

assessing conformance upon receipt isn't feasible due to:

the product containing critical characteristics not visible in the end item

special safety or security characteristics

risk of damage to special packing and packaging

delivery to multiple locations

inadequate conditions or capabilities at the destination

The GQA at source may be performed by an inspection authority specified by the client or by an inspection authority commissioned by PSPC on the client's behalf. As part of the inspection, contractor performance data respecting quality must be documented and copies of all inspection reports provided to the sector or region.

Additionally, PSPC holds the authority to provide additional quality tasking for civilian marine services, such as conducting inspections and arranging for technical support.

Listing programs

You can expedite procurement by using listing programs. This establishes a list of pre-identified products or services that comply with recognized performance standards or specifications. These lists are developed and maintained by the CGSB and the Department of National Defence (DND).

Listing or qualification programs are normally established in situations where:

testing requirements could delay delivery

the costs of acceptance inspection are prohibitively high

assurance of product conformance or offeror capability is needed beforehand

complex testing equipment and procedures are required

there are regular purchases of products in large quantities

Before contracting, you should verify which product or service offered has been approved with the standards (listing) organization. This signifies compliance with recognized standards or specifications, however, it does not relieve the contractor from meeting all specified contract requirements or guarantee acceptance under a contract.

Reasons that the responsible qualifying authority may discontinue the qualification and remove a product or service from an existing list include:

changes in product formulation that impair product quality

production process adjustments that impair product quality

verified, authenticated field failures, which is attributable to non-conformance of the product to the relevant standard or specification (in contrast with perceived field failures which should be reported by users to the qualifying authority)

failure to meet verification test requirements

failure to submit samples for testing, where requested

failure to submit data for qualification maintenance, when requested

withdrawal for cause, such as the contractor has ceased operation, changed location or has consistently failed to respond to requests for quotation

a cancelled listing resulting from a cancelled governing standard, or a cancelled or superseded specification

When there are indications of non-conformance, and if PSPC and the client determine that a qualified offeror or contractor does not conform to the applicable standard, you must notify the qualifying authority. Offerors or contractors have the right to appeal against the removal as per the established appeal procedures.

When utilizing a listing program for procurement, you must state in the notice of proposed procurement (NPP), solicitation of offers, and contract documents that the offeror or contractor and its product must be listed in the appropriate listing as detailed below.

QPL includes:

underlay

security guards

remanufactured toner cartridges

protective clothing

polyethylene vapor barrier

paints

office furniture

medical gloves

Laboratory Acceptance Program

dockside Monitoring Company

carpets

Canadian Non-Destructive Testing Personnel Certification Program

Canadian Air Transport Security Authority screening contractors

breather type sheathing membrane

CPL includes:

polyethylene vapor barrier

surgical and patient examination rubber gloves

breather type sheathing membrane

firefighter's protective clothing, protecting against heat and flame

fireline workwear for forest firefighters

Laboratory Acceptance Program

Registered Quality Systems List ISO 9001 (ISO 9000 Quality Management Systems):

Lists of companies compliant with ISO 9001:2015 models for quality systems, marking the transition from ISO 9001:2008 to the current version.

Registered Environmental Management Systems List:

Lists of companies compliant with ISO 14001:2015 standard for environmental management systems.

Certified Occupational Health and Safety Standards (OHSAS) List:

Lists of companies compliant with OHSAS 18001:1999 specification for health and safety management systems.

Certified Food Safety Management Systems List: Alberta and Ontario Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) Advantage List (AHA!) Certification Program: the formalized process whereby a food safety management system is assessed against the AHA! standard of the Alberta Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.

Ontario HACCP Advantage Certification Program: the formalized process whereby a food safety management system is assessed against the Advantage HACCP standard of the Ontario Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs.

Certified Drinking Water Quality Systems (Ministry of Ontario) List:

A registration program designed to recognize operating authorities managing municipal residential drinking water systems that meet the Ontario Drinking Water Quality Standard through accreditation by an independent third party.

Certified Occupational Health and Safety Standards (OHSAS) List:

Lists of companies compliant with OHSAS 18001:1999 specification for health and safety management systems.

Certified Food Safety Management Systems List: Alberta and Ontario Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) Advantage List (AHA!) Certification Program: the formalized process whereby a food safety management system is assessed against the AHA! standard of the Alberta Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development.

Ontario HACCP Advantage Certification Program: the formalized process whereby a food safety management system is assessed against the Advantage HACCP standard of the Ontario Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs.

Certified Drinking Water Quality Systems (Ministry of Ontario) List:

A registration program designed to recognize operating authorities managing municipal residential drinking water systems that meet the Ontario Drinking Water Quality Standard through accreditation by an independent third party.
